name: AFFIRM-LITE fisetin frailty trial is enrolling by invitation with no posted results
summary: AFFIRM-LITE is direct fisetin trial-watch evidence for Senolytics, but it cannot support efficacy because results are not posted.
statement: ClinicalTrials.gov lists NCT03675724 as an enrolling-by-invitation phase 2 randomized double-blind fisetin trial in older adults with estimated enrollment of 40, blood inflammation marker primary endpoint, estimated primary completion in November 2026, estimated study completion in November 2027, and no posted results in the June 11, 2026 API view.
population_or_model: Older adults in a fisetin frailty and inflammation trial
time_horizon: Estimated primary completion November 2026 and study completion November 2027
quantitative_note: Estimated enrollment is 40; randomized allocation; double masking; primary outcome is decrease in blood inflammation markers.
caveats: No-results registry status is not evidence of benefit or harm.
Inflammation-marker movement would still need functional and safety interpretation.
Fisetin exposure, formulation, and tissue target engagement remain open questions.
